title: dc_concepts
description: Derivation Concept model schema: categories of derivation concepts with shared dimensions and FHIR-aligned result types. SKOS-inspired taxonomy structure. Validates DC vocabulary files (Option A-D variants).

version.description: Semantic version of the DC concept model

description.description: Human-readable description of the model (SKOS: skos:note)

namingPrinciples.description: Guiding principles for naming derivation concepts

sharedDimensions.description: Dimensional concepts defining the context in which derivations are performed. Not derivation concepts themselves — selected by the Transformation.

categories.description: Derivation categories, each containing related derivation concepts (SKOS: skos:Collection)

conceptProxies.description: Optional: maps app-level pseudo-categories (e.g. 'Observation') to a DC concept that should stand in for them during transformation-matching lookups.

ResultValueType.description: Semantic value type of a derivation result. Clinical naming with FHIR alignment: NumericValue ≈ Quantity, CharacterValue ≈ string, CodedResponse ≈ CodeableConcept.
ResultValueType.title: ResultValueType

ConceptProxies.description: Registry mapping pseudo-category names (e.g. 'Observation') to a DC concept that stands in for them during transformation-matching lookups. Proxy selection is governed by result.valueType compatibility.

proxyConcept.description: Name of the DC concept (from categories[*].concepts) that should be used as a proxy.

rationale.description: Human-readable explanation of why this proxy mapping exists.

SharedDimension.description: A dimensional concept that defines context for derivations (e.g. Subject, Timing, Treatment). Selected by the Transformation, mapped to physical columns by the Implementation.

cardinality.description: How many instances of this dimension apply to a derivation

autoConfirm.description: When true, terminal derivation pipeline slots whose concept matches this dimension name are automatically marked as confirmed source-data dimensions, without user interaction.

DerivationConcept.description: A single derivation concept: describes what kind of data the result IS, not how it is produced. Each concept produces a result of one or more value types (NumericValue, CharacterValue, CodedResponse). SKOS: skos:Concept, narrower than its DerivationCategory.

mathematicalEquivalent.description: Abstract mathematical name for this concept (e.g. AbsoluteDifference, SequenceMaximum)

math.description: Mathematical formula or expression (e.g. x − x_ref, sup{yᵢ}, ∫y dt)

examples.description: Concrete clinical examples of this derivation

result.description: Result value type and unit behaviour. The valueType connects this concept to one or more ResultValueType nodes (NumericValue, CharacterValue, CodedResponse).

valueType.description: The value type(s) this derivation produces — connects to ResultValueType. Single type or array of allowed types.

unit.description: Unit behaviour (e.g. inherited, fixed: %, dimensionless, none, time unit)

DerivationCategory.description: A named group of related derivation concepts (SKOS: skos:Collection). Defines the input pattern and contains the concept map.

definition.description: Human-readable definition of this derivation category (SKOS: skos:definition)

inputPattern.description: Whether inputs are method-driven or concept-driven, and what the category requires

concepts.description: Map of concept name (SKOS: prefLabel) to DerivationConcept definition
